Use these dashboard visuals for your project:

1. KPI Cards

Keep top row:

  • Network Traffic

  • Alerts Count

2. Main Visuals

CPU Usage Trend
Line chart by Date/Time

Memory Usage Trend
Area chart by Date/Time

Server Status
Donut chart: Active, Warning, Down

Alerts by Severity
Bar chart: Critical, High, Medium, Low

Cloud Cost by Service
Column chart: Compute, Storage, Network, Database

Region-wise Server Load
Map visual or bar chart by Region

Top 10 High CPU Servers
Horizontal bar chart

Downtime by Server
Stacked bar chart

3. Table Visual

Detailed table:

  • Server ID

  • Region

  • CPU %

  • Memory %

  • Status

  • Alert Type

  • Cost

  • Last Checked Time

4. Filters / Slicers

Keep left side:

  • Date

  • Region

  • Server Type

  • Status

  • Severity

  • Cloud Provider

Best Dashboard Title

Cloud Infrastructure Monitoring Dashboard

Page Names

  1. Overview

  2. Performance Monitoring

  3. Alerts & Incidents

  4. Cost Analysis

Your dataset is perfect for a professional Cloud Infrastructure Monitoring Dashboard.
Here’s the best dashboard layout and visuals for each sheet.

Dashboard Structure

Page 1 — Executive Overview

KPI Cards

Keep these at the top:

  • Total Servers

Recommended Visuals

Infrastructure by Region

Donut Chart

  • Legend → Region

Environment Distribution

You said:

error raised here

ChatGPT said:

This error is from Power Query data type mismatch.

Your column Detected_Time contains DateTime values, but Power BI is trying to convert it to Text.

Fix

  1. Go to Power Query Editor

  2. Select column Detected_Time

  3. Click Data Type dropdown

  4. Change it to Date/Time

  5. Click Replace current

  6. Click Close & Apply

If it still shows error

In Power Query, go to Advanced Editor and find a step like:

powerquery
Table.TransformColumnTypes(... {"Detected_Time", type text} ...)

Change it to:

powerquery
Table.TransformColumnTypes(... {"Detected_Time", type datetime} ...)

Correct format

Your value:

22-03-2026 00:32:56

should be treated as:

Date/Time

not Text.

After fixing, the Error values will disappear.


You said:

give me metrics on this datset

ChatGPT said:

For your Cloud Infrastructure Monitoring Dataset, these are the best professional Power BI Metrics (Measures/KPIs) you should create.

Infrastructure Metrics

1. Total Servers

DAX
Total Servers = DISTINCTCOUNT(Infrastructure[Server_ID])

2. Average CPU Usage

DAX
Avg CPU Usage = AVERAGE(Infrastructure[CPU_Usage_Percent])

3. Average Memory Usage

DAX
Avg Memory Usage = AVERAGE(Infrastructure[Memory_Usage_Percent])

4. Average Disk Usage

DAX
Avg Disk Usage = AVERAGE(Infrastructure[Disk_Usage_Percent])

5. Average Network Traffic

DAX
Avg Network Traffic =
AVERAGE(Infrastructure[Network_In_MB]) +
AVERAGE(Infrastructure[Network_Out_MB])

Incident Metrics

6. Total Incidents

DAX
Total Incidents = COUNT(Incidents[Incident_ID])

7. Critical Incidents

DAX
Critical Incidents =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Incidents[Incident_ID]),
    Incidents[Severity] = "Critical"
)

8. Average Downtime

DAX
Avg Downtime =
AVERAGE(Incidents[Downtime_Minutes])

9. Resolved Incidents

DAX
Resolved Incidents =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Incidents[Incident_ID]),
    Incidents[Status] = "Resolved"
)

Security Metrics

10. Total Security Threats

DAX
Total Threats =
COUNT(Security[Security_Event_ID])

11. High Severity Threats

DAX
High Severity Threats =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Security[Security_Event_ID]),
    Security[Severity] = "High"
)

12. Blocked Threats

DAX
Blocked Threats =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Security[Security_Event_ID]),
    Security[Action_Taken] = "Blocked"
)

Cost Metrics

13. Total Cloud Cost

DAX
Total Cloud Cost =
SUM(Costs[Daily_Cost_USD])

14. Average Daily Cost

DAX
Avg Daily Cost =
AVERAGE(Costs[Daily_Cost_USD])

15. Highest Cost Region

DAX
Highest Cost Region =
TOPN(
    1,
    VALUES(Costs[Region]),
    [Total Cloud Cost],
    DESC
)

SLA Metrics

16. SLA Availability %

DAX
Avg SLA Availability =
AVERAGE(SLA[Availability_Percent])

17. SLA Breach Count

DAX
SLA Breaches =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(SLA[SLA_ID]),
    SLA[Availability_Percent] < 99
)

Deployment Metrics

18. Successful Deployments

DAX
Successful Deployments =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Deployments[Deployment_ID]),
    Deployments[Deployment_Status] = "Success"
)

19. Failed Deployments

DAX
Failed Deployments =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Deployments[Deployment_ID]),
    Deployments[Deployment_Status] = "Failed"
)

20. Deployment Success Rate

DAX
Deployment Success Rate =
DIVIDE(
    [Successful Deployments],
    COUNT(Deployments[Deployment_ID]),
    0
) * 100

Advanced Professional Metrics

21. Server Health Score

DAX
Health Score =
100 -
(
    AVERAGE(Infrastructure[CPU_Usage_Percent]) * 0.4 +
    AVERAGE(Infrastructure[Memory_Usage_Percent]) * 0.3 +
    AVERAGE(Infrastructure[Disk_Usage_Percent]) * 0.3
)

22. Incident Resolution Rate

DAX
Resolution Rate =
DIVIDE(
    [Resolved Incidents],
    [Total Incidents],
    0
) * 100

23. Threat Resolution Rate

DAX
Threat Resolution Rate =
DIVIDE(
    [Blocked Threats],
    [Total Threats],
    0
) * 100

Best KPI Cards to Keep

Top Row:

  • Total Servers

  • Avg CPU Usage

  • Total Incidents

  • Critical Incidents

  • Total Threats

  • Total Cloud Cost

  • SLA Availability %

  • Deployment Success Rate

These metrics will make your dashboard look like a real enterprise monitoring solution.


You said:

cloud infrastructure monitoring dataset
Server_IDHostnameRegion
SRV-100001cloud-node-1us-east-1
SRV-100002cloud-node-2us-east-1
SRV-100003cloud-node-3us-west-2
SRV-100004cloud-node-4us-west-2
SRV-100005cloud-node-5eu-central-1
SRV-100006cloud-node-6ap-southeast-1
give me measures on this data set

ChatGPT said:

Create these Power BI DAX measures.

Infrastructure

DAX
Total Servers = DISTINCTCOUNT(Infrastructure_Inventory[Server_ID])

Avg Uptime % = AVERAGE(Infrastructure_Inventory[Uptime_Percentage])

Total Monthly Cost = SUM(Infrastructure_Inventory[Monthly_Cost_USD])

Total CPU Cores = SUM(Infrastructure_Inventory[CPU_Cores])

Total RAM GB = SUM(Infrastructure_Inventory[RAM_GB])

Total Storage GB = SUM(Infrastructure_Inventory[Storage_GB])

Monitoring

DAX
Avg CPU % = AVERAGE(Monitoring_Metrics[CPU_Usage_Percent])

Avg Memory % = AVERAGE(Monitoring_Metrics[Memory_Usage_Percent])

Avg Disk % = AVERAGE(Monitoring_Metrics[Disk_Usage_Percent])

Avg Response Time = AVERAGE(Monitoring_Metrics[Response_Time_ms])

Avg Error Rate % = AVERAGE(Monitoring_Metrics[Error_Rate_Percent])

Total Network MB =
SUM(Monitoring_Metrics[Network_In_MB]) +
SUM(Monitoring_Metrics[Network_Out_MB])

Incidents

DAX
Total Incidents = COUNT(Incidents[Incident_ID])

Open Incidents =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Incidents[Incident_ID]),
    Incidents[Status] = "Open"
)

Critical Incidents =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Incidents[Incident_ID]),
    Incidents[Severity] = "Critical"
)

Avg Downtime Minutes = AVERAGE(Incidents[Downtime_Minutes])

Total Downtime Hours =
DIVIDE(SUM(Incidents[Downtime_Minutes]), 60)

Resolved Incidents =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Incidents[Incident_ID]),
    Incidents[Status] = "Resolved"
)

Incident Resolution Rate % =
DIVIDE([Resolved Incidents], [Total Incidents], 0) * 100

Deployments

DAX
Total Deployments = COUNT(Deployments[Deployment_ID])

Successful Deployments =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Deployments[Deployment_ID]),
    Deployments[Deployment_Status] = "Success"
)

Failed Deployments =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Deployments[Deployment_ID]),
    Deployments[Deployment_Status] = "Failed"
)

Rollback Deployments =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Deployments[Deployment_ID]),
    Deployments[Deployment_Status] = "Rollback"
)

Deployment Success Rate % =
DIVIDE([Successful Deployments], [Total Deployments], 0) * 100

Avg Deployment Duration =
AVERAGE(Deployments[Deployment_Duration_Minutes])

Cloud Costs

DAX
Total Cloud Cost = SUM(Cloud_Costs[Daily_Cost_USD])

Avg Daily Cost = AVERAGE(Cloud_Costs[Daily_Cost_USD])

Total Data Transfer GB = SUM(Cloud_Costs[Data_Transfer_GB])

Total Storage Used GB = SUM(Cloud_Costs[Storage_Used_GB])

Cost Per GB Transfer =
DIVIDE([Total Cloud Cost], [Total Data Transfer GB], 0)

Security

DAX
Total Security Events = COUNT(Security_Events[Security_Event_ID])

Critical Threats =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Security_Events[Security_Event_ID]),
    Security_Events[Severity] = "Critical"
)

Blocked Threats =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Security_Events[Security_Event_ID]),
    Security_Events[Action_Taken] = "Blocked"
)

Resolved Threats =
CALCULATE(
    COUNT(Security_Events[Security_Event_ID]),
    Security_Events[Action_Taken] = "Resolved"
)

Threat Block Rate % =
DIVIDE([Blocked Threats], [Total Security Events], 0) * 100

SLA

DAX
Avg SLA Availability % = AVERAGE(SLA_Availability[Availability_Percent])

Avg Latency ms = AVERAGE(SLA_Availability[Latency_ms])

SLA Breach Count =
CALCULATE(
    COUNTROWS(SLA_Availability),
    SLA_Availability[Availability_Percent] < SLA_Availability[SLA_Target_Percent]
)

SLA Compliance Rate % =
DIVIDE(
    COUNTROWS(SLA_Availability) - [SLA Breach Count],
    COUNTROWS(SLA_Availability),
    0
) * 100

Best KPI Cards

Use these on dashboard top:

DAX
Total Servers
Avg CPU %
Total Incidents
Critical Incidents
Total Security Events
Total Cloud Cost
Avg SLA Availability %
Deployment Success Rate %

ChatGPT said:

For your Total Servers KPI Card, use this professional setup.

DAX Measure

DAX
Total Servers =
DISTINCTCOUNT(Infrastructure_Inventory[Server_ID])

KPI Card Design

6

Card Settings

SettingValue
Background#1C2541
Text ColorWhite
Accent Color#3A86FF
Border Radius15px
ShadowSoft Glow
FontBahnschrift Bold

KPI Layout

Title

TOTAL SERVERS

Icon

Use:

  • Server icon

  • Cloud server icon

  • Database icon

Best Conditional Logic

Healthy

If servers > 90%

  • Green indicator

Warning

If servers between 70–90%

  • Orange

Critical

If below 70%

  • Red

Add Small Trend Indicator

Example:

↑ 5.2% from last month

Use:

  • Green arrow for increase

  • Red arrow for decrease

Best Card Size

Width: 220
Height: 120

Best Power BI Visual

Use:

New Card Visual

or

Card with States (custom visual)

Professional Tooltip

  • Total Active Servers

  • Total Inactive Servers

or

You said:

where can i apply of kpi card total servers

ChatGPT said:

Apply the Total Servers KPI Card on the top-left side of your dashboard.

This is the best professional placement:

7

Recommended KPI Order (Top Row)

PositionKPI
1Total Servers
2Avg CPU Usage
3Avg Memory Usage
4Total Incidents
5Critical Threats
6SLA Availability %
7Deployment Success Rate
8Total Cloud Cost

Why Total Servers First?

Because it represents:

  • Total infrastructure scale

  • Main asset count

  • Overall environment size

It acts as the primary infrastructure KPI.

Best Placement

TOP LEFT

[ Total Servers ] [ CPU ] [ Memory ] [ Incidents ]

This follows enterprise dashboard standards.

Best Visual Pairing

Below the Total Servers KPI card, keep:

  • Infrastructure by Region

  • Server Health Status

  • CPU Trend

This creates logical flow.

Recommended Icon

Use:

  • Server Rack

  • Cloud Server

  • Database Stack

Best Card Color

Primary KPI

Use blue highlight:

#3A86FF

This makes the first KPI stand out.

Additional Professional Touch

Add:

Last Refreshed: TODAY()

small text under KPI row.

Best Dashboard Flow

TOP

KPI Cards

MIDDLE

Performance Trends

LOWER

Security + Cost + Incident Analytics

ChatGPT said:

No problem. Here’s the easiest step-by-step way to build the Total Servers KPI Card in Power BI.

Step 1 — Load Dataset

Open:

Power BI Desktop

Then:

Home → Get Data → Excel → Select Dataset

Load all tables.

Step 2 — Create Measure

Go to:

Modeling → New Measure

Paste this:

DAX
Total Servers =
DISTINCTCOUNT(Infrastructure_Inventory[Server_ID])

Press:

Enter

Measure created successfully.

Step 3 — Add KPI Card Visual

Go to:

Visualizations Panel

Click:

Card Visual

It looks like:

123

Step 4 — Add Measure into Card

From Fields panel:
Drag:

Total Servers

into:

Values

Now your KPI card appears.

Step 5 — Format Professionally

Click KPI Card → Go to:

Format Visual (Paint Brush Icon)

Apply These Settings

Callout Value

SettingValue
Font Size28–36
ColorWhite
FontBahnschrift Bold

Category Label

Turn ON

SettingValue
TextTOTAL SERVERS
ColorWhite
Font Size14

Background

Turn ON

SettingValue
Color#1C2541
Transparency10%

Border

Turn ON

SettingValue
Radius15px

Shadow

Turn ON

Soft shadow effect.

Final Result

Your KPI card will look like this:

4

Best Position

Keep it:

Top Left Corner

of dashboard.

Next KPI Cards to Create

After Total Servers:

  1. Avg CPU Usage

  2. Avg Memory Usage

  3. Total Incidents

  4. Critical Threats

  5. Total Cloud Cost

These create a professional dashboard row.

You said:

i need to build kpi

ChatGPT said:

Great. Let’s build a real KPI Visual now (not Card visual).

A KPI visual needs 3 things:

  1. Indicator (main value)

  2. Trend Axis (date/time)

  3. Target Goal

STEP-BY-STEP KPI BUILD

Step 1 — Select KPI Visual

Choose this icon:

7

It looks like:

⬆ 123

Step 2 — Create Measures

Go:

Modeling → New Measure

Main KPI Measure

DAX
Total Servers =
DISTINCTCOUNT(Infrastructure_Inventory[Server_ID])

Target Measure

DAX
Target Servers = 100

Step 3 — Add Fields

Indicator

Drag:

Total Servers

into:

Indicator

Trend Axis

Drag:

Timestamp

from Monitoring_Metrics table into:

Trend Axis

Target Goals

Drag:

Target Servers

into:

Target goals

FINAL RESULT

Now KPI visual shows:

  • Total server count

  • Trend over time

  • Target comparison

BEST KPI SETTINGS

Go:

Format Visual

Apply:

SettingValue
Background#1C2541
Indicator ColorWhite
Trend Color#3A86FF
Goal ColorGreen
Font Size28
Border Radius15

Professional KPI Example

4

Important Difference

VisualUse
CardSingle number only
KPINumber + trend + target

Best KPIs to Build

After this build:

  1. Total Servers

  2. Avg CPU Usage

  3. SLA Availability %

  4. Deployment Success Rate

  5. Total Incidents

  6. Critical Threats

You said:

how can i keep logo to every visual

ChatGPT said:

To keep your company logo on every visual/page in Power BI professionally, follow this method.

BEST METHOD (Professional)

Add Logo in Header Area

Do NOT place logo inside every chart.

Instead:

  • Keep one logo at top-left

  • Visible on all pages

  • Looks clean and enterprise-level

Like this:

4

STEP-BY-STEP

Method 1 — Same Logo on Every Page (Recommended)

Step 1

Go:

Insert → Image

Step 2

Select your logo PNG.

Use:

  • Transparent background logo

  • PNG format

Step 3

Place logo:

Top Left Corner

Recommended size:

SettingValue
Width120–160 px
Height50–70 px

Step 4

Copy logo

Press:

CTRL + C

Step 5

Go to next page

Press:

CTRL + V

Repeat on all pages.

BEST POSITION

Recommended Layout

[ LOGO ]      Dashboard Title

Keep:

  • Logo left

  • Dashboard title center/top

BEST HEADER SIZE

ElementHeight
Header Bar70–90 px

HEADER COLOR

Use:

#081028

or

#1C2541

BEST PROFESSIONAL STYLE

Add:

  • Slight glow shadow

  • Transparent background

  • Rounded edges

DO NOT

❌ Do not place logo inside:

  • Pie charts

  • Treemaps

  • Line charts

It makes dashboard look crowded.

BEST ENTERPRISE DESIGN

Keep only:

  • One logo

  • One title

  • One navigation bar

This is how professional dashboards are designed.

OPTIONAL PREMIUM STYLE

You can also create:

  • Left vertical navigation panel

  • Logo at top

  • Navigation buttons below

Like:

LOGO
Overview
Infrastructure
Security
Incidents
Costs
SLA

This looks very advanced.

BEST TITLE COMBINATION

Logo + Title

CLOUDGUARD
Infrastructure Monitoring & Security

BEST FONT FOR TITLE

UsageFont
Logo TextBahnschrift Bold
SubtitleSegoe UI

EXTRA PROFESSIONAL TOUCH

Add:

Last Refreshed: TODAY()

small text on top-right.

This gives enterprise feel.
